headline: KL Flash Floods: 47 Events in 2024 Break 30-Year Record
context: DBKL recorded 47 flash flood events in Kuala Lumpur in 2024, the highest since records began in 1994, caused by intensifying rainfall and drainage systems designed for 1980s capacity.

cards.big: 47 flash floods hit KL in 2024 — a 30-year record.
cards.sub: The drainage system was designed in the 1980s for rainfall intensities 35% below current patterns.

cards.big: SMART Tunnel operates at full capacity during only 12% of flood events.
cards.sub: The tunnel was designed for river flooding, not surface flash floods. It addresses the wrong problem in 88% of cases.

cards.big: RM2.8B in property damage from KL floods between 2022 and 2024.
cards.sub: Annual damage now exceeds annual infrastructure spending on flood mitigation.

cards.big: KL's impervious surface area increased from 58% to 74% since 2000.
cards.sub: Every new development that paves green space accelerates runoff. No mandatory retention requirement exists.

cards.big: A drainage system designed for 1980s rain serving a 2024 city. The infrastructure is 40 years behind the climate.
KL needs mandatory on-site water retention for all new developments, upgraded trunk drainage, and permeable surface requirements. The current approach — responding to each flood — is not adaptation.
